revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:民俗文化財]]
{{otheruseslist|Japan's folk cultural properties|Other folk cultural properties|Folk cultural properties (disambiguation)}} {{Referencesの明記|date=2020th year 4月}}
'''Folk Cultural Properties''' (民俗文化財, Minzoku Bunkazai) refer to {{ill|folk materials|ja|民俗資料|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/folk materials|1=folk materials|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11547891}} that are particularly significant for their documentary value and require preservation measures or are expected to benefit from such measures as part of the cultural heritage protection system implemented by the {{ill|Country|en|Country|qid=Q6256|lt=nation|1=Country}} or {{ill|local governments|ja|地方公共団体|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/local governments|1=local public entity|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q27116296}}.
This article primarily discusses cases in Japan.
== Types ==
Folk cultural properties in Japan are broadly classified into '''Tangible Folk Cultural Properties''' and '''Intangible Folk Cultural Properties'''.
Each category has its own designation systems: '''{{ill|Important Tangible Folk Cultural Properties|ja|重要有形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Tangible Folk Cultural Properties|1=National Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11644858}}''' and '''{{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|1=National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q6573893}}'''. Additionally, there are systems to complement these, such as '''{{ill|Registered Tangible Folk Cultural Properties|ja|登録有形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered Tangible Folk Cultural Properties|1=Registered Tangible Folk Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11579196}}''', '''{{ill|Registered Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|ja|登録無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|1=Registered Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q112228211}}''', and '''{{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Properties Requiring Documentation|lt=Intangible Folk Cultural Properties Requiring Documentation|lt=Intangible Folk Cultural Properties requiring measures for documentation|ja|記録作成等の措置を講ずべき無形の民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Properties requiring measures for documentation|qq=}}'''.
The methods of protection and handling differ among these types.
== Development of the Folk Cultural Property System in Japan ==
In Japan, folk cultural properties became targets of protection under the {{ill|Cultural Property Protection Act|ja|文化財保護法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cultural Property Protection Act|1=Cultural Properties Protection Law|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1188502}} enacted in {{ill|1950|en|1950|qid=Q18597|lt=1950|1=1950}} (Showa 25). At that time, current folk cultural properties were classified as "folk materials" and included under {{ill|Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)|qid=Q2901860|lt=Tangible Cultural Properties|1=Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)}}, alongside "buildings" and "artworks." The 1954 amendment to the Act (commonly referred to as the "first revision") separated the protection system for tangible folk materials from the designation system for tangible cultural properties, launching the designation system for "Important Folk Materials." At the same time, a selection system for "Intangible Folk Materials requiring measures for documentation" was introduced.
Subsequent amendments in {{ill|1975|en|1975|qid=Q2479|lt=1975|1=1975}} (Showa 50) renamed "folk materials" to "folk cultural properties," positioning the existing "Important Folk Materials" as "Important Tangible Folk Cultural Properties" and introducing the designation system for "Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties." Further amendments in {{ill|2005|en|2005|qid=Q2019|lt=2005|1=2005}} (Heisei 17) established the registration system for tangible folk cultural properties, and in {{ill|2021|en|2021|qid=Q49628|lt=2021|1=2021}} (Reiwa 3), a registration system for intangible folk cultural properties was introduced.
The current Cultural Property Protection Act defines folk cultural properties in Article 2, Clause 1, Item 3 as follows: * Customs related to clothing, food, housing, livelihood, faith, annual events, etc.; folk performing arts; folk techniques; and related items such as clothing, tools, houses, and other objects that are indispensable for understanding the transitions in the life of the Japanese people.
This definition builds upon the wording from the 1954 amendment, with the additions of "folk performing arts" (1975) and "folk techniques" (2004)<ref name="hogohou50" />. Folk techniques refer to the techniques for producing tools and items necessary for daily life and production<ref name="hogohou50">[[#Protection Act|『Latest Amendment to the Cultural Property Protection Act』 (2006) pp.50-55]]</ref><ref name="hogohou110">[[#Protection Act|『Latest Amendment to the Cultural Property Protection Act』 (2006) pp.110-115]]</ref>{{refnest|group="Notes"|Examples of folk techniques that should be preserved include the {{ill|construction techniques of Japanese boats|ja|和船製作技術|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/construction techniques of Japanese boats|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the Tsugaru Strait region ({{ill|Aomori Prefecture|en|Aomori Prefecture|qid=Q71699|lt=Aomori Prefecture|1=Aomori Prefecture}}), the {{ill|Kazusa digging techniques|ja|上総掘り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazusa digging techniques|1=Kazusa-bori method|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11360037}} in {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|qid=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}}, and the {{ill|Sinter production technique|ja|湯の花製造技術|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sinter production technique|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 湯の花|1=Sinter|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11563426}} in {{ill|Beppu Onsen|en|Beppu Onsen|qid=Q4890932|lt=Beppu's Myoban Hot Springs|1=Beppu Onsen}} ({{ill|Ōita Prefecture|en|Ōita Prefecture|qid=Q133924|lt=Oita Prefecture|1=Ōita Prefecture}})<ref name="hogohou50" />.}}.
== Tangible Folk Cultural Properties ==
=== Important Tangible Folk Cultural Properties === In Japan, the nation designates particularly important tangible folk cultural properties as '''Important Tangible Folk Cultural Properties''' and implements preservation measures. As of March 22, 2023, 226 items have been designated, categorized as follows: * Clothing, food, and housing (29 items) * Production and livelihood (99 items) * Transportation and communication (19 items) * Trade (1 item) * Social life (1 item) * Faith (41 items) * Folk knowledge (7 items) * Folk performing arts, entertainment, and games (23 items) * Life events (3 items) * Annual events (3 items)
=== Registered Tangible Folk Cultural Properties === The 2004 amendment to the Cultural Property Protection Act introduced a registration system for folk cultural properties. Tangible folk cultural properties not designated by the nation or local governments but deemed in need of preservation and utilization are registered as '''Registered Tangible Folk Cultural Properties'''. As of March 22, 2023, 49 items have been registered.
== References ==
=== Notes === {{Reflist|group=Notes}} === Citations === {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|author=文化財保護法研究会（編著）|year=2006|title=最新改正 文化財保護法|publisher=[[Gyōsei]]|isbn=4-324-07873-4|ref=Protection Act}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=文化庁内民俗文化財研究会（編著）|year=1979|title=民俗文化財の手引き|publisher=[[Daiichi Hōki]]|isbn=4-474-06110-1|ref=Guide}}
== External Links == * [https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/document?lawid=325AC0100000214 Cultural Property Protection Act] * [https://www.bunka.go.jp/ Agency for Cultural Affairs]
{{Cultural Properties of Japan}}
